operating_state: Total net sales increased 13.3% to $167.7 billion in Q2 2025, driven by AWS growth of 17.5% and double-digit expansion across North America and International segments with favorable foreign exchange impact. Operating income expanded 30.6% to $19.2 billion reflecting improved operational leverage across all segments. Six-month revenue reached $323.4 billion, up 11.0% year-over-year, demonstrating consistent expansion.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 11.4% to $148.5 billion, demonstrating improved cost leverage as expense growth lagged revenue expansion by approximately 200 basis points. Technology and infrastructure costs surged 22% primarily from AWS infrastructure investments and accelerated server depreciation, while fulfillment costs rose from increased sales volume partially offset by network efficiencies.
material_events: Amazon changed server and networking equipment useful life from six to five years effective January 2025, increasing Q2 depreciation expense by $280 million. The company invested $1.3 billion in Anthropic convertible notes with an additional $1.4 billion commitment by Q4 2025, having previously realized a $3.9 billion gain from converting notes to preferred stock in Q1 2025. Capital expenditures increased substantially to $31.4 billion from $16.4 billion primarily for technology infrastructure supporting AWS growth.
macro_exposures: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 reinstated 100% accelerated depreciation and immediate R&D expensing retroactively, expected to significantly decrease U.S. cash taxes while increasing income tax provision. Foreign exchange volatility presents ongoing risk with substantial foreign-denominated cash and securities exposed to potential declines. Increased regulatory scrutiny includes ongoing Federal Trade Commission antitrust proceedings while expanding global tax obligations include new revenue-based taxes and global minimum tax implementations.
forward_outlook: Q3 2025 guidance projects net sales between $174.0-179.5 billion representing 10-13% growth with favorable foreign exchange impact anticipated, and operating income expected between $15.5-20.5 billion. Management expects technology and infrastructure spending to increase over time supporting product and service expansion. AWS maintains substantial unrecognized performance obligations with weighted-average remaining contract life of 4.0 years.
uncertainty_profile: Income tax contingencies totaled $5.8 billion with ongoing IRS examinations and multiple international tax disputes including contested Indian cloud services assessments. Substantial legal contingencies from expanding litigation portfolio include patent litigation regarding AWS Nitro System technology. Evolving artificial intelligence regulations and data protection requirements create compliance complexity, while semiconductor supply constraints for AI infrastructure components pose risks to technology development timelines.
